title: 10 best herbal creams for gastroenteritis

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with gastroenteritis because it contains calendula, a natural anti-inflammatory agent that soothes and calms irritated mucous membranes in the digestive tract. The cream's emollient properties also help to restore the integrity of the gut lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with diarrhea and stomach cramps. Additionally, calendula's antimicrobial properties may help combat underlying infections, promoting a faster recovery from gastroenteritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Pot marigold contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms like diarrhea and abdominal pain.

Antimicrobial activity: The cream's antimicrobial properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as E. coli and Salmonella, can help to combat the underlying infection causing gastroenteritis, promoting a faster recovery.

Soothing and protective mucilage: Pot marigold's mucilage contains soothing agents that protect and calm the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with diarrhea, cramps, and bloating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant contain flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in soothing digestive issues related to gastroenteritis.
Barks: The bark of the pot marigold plant contains triterpenoids, which have antiviral and antibacterial properties, helping to combat infections associated with gastroenteritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality calendula flowers and dry them thoroughly using a food dehydrator for 6 hours.
Grind the dried calendula flowers into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 3 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of calendula powder with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass measuring cup.
Gently warm the mixture by placing it in a double boiler over simmering water for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter into a clean glass jar and store it in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with gastroenteritis because of its unique ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. The anti-inflammatory properties in aloe vera help reduce inflammation in the gut, easing symptoms such as abdominal pain, cramping, and diarrhea. Additionally, aloe's moisturizing effects can help restore the natural balance of the gut lining, promoting healing and reducing inflammation. This natural remedy provides quick relief from gastroenteritis symptoms, making it an effective treatment option for many people suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms such as abdominal pain and cramping.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial agents present in aloe vera cream, such as aloe-emodin, can help combat bacterial overgrowth in the gut, reducing diarrhea and other symptoms of gastroenteritis.

Healing and protective property: Aloe vera's gel contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as other nutrients that promote wound healing and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, accelerating the recovery process from gastroenteritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help soothe and calm digestive issues associated with gastroenteritis.
Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation and pain in the gastrointestinal tract.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried peppermint leaves and dried ginger root measuring one teaspoon each.
Crush the dried peppermint leaves and dried ginger root into a fine powder using mortar and pestle for five minutes.
Mix two tablespoons of cooled distilled water with one tablespoon of aloe vera gel in a glass bowl until well combined taking ten seconds.
Add the powdered peppermint and ginger mixture to the aloe vera mixture stirring clockwise for thirty seconds.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for one hour before transferring to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with gastroenteritis because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that soothe the digestive tract. The cream's topical application allows for direct delivery of these active ingredients to the affected area, providing rapid relief from symptoms such as nausea, stomach cramps, and diarrhea. Ginger's warming properties also help stimulate digestion, reducing inflammation and promoting a sense of calm in the gut. As a result, herbal ginger creams can provide effective and natural relief from the discomforts associated with gastroenteritis.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, help reduce inflammation and swelling in the digestive tract, which can alleviate symptoms of gastroenteritis like abdominal pain and nausea.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial properties of ginger, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, can help combat the underlying infection that causes gastroenteritis, reducing the duration and severity of symptoms.

Stomach soothing property: Ginger's natural compounds, such as volatile oils and phenolic acids, have been shown to soothe the stomach lining, reduce inflammation, and increase digestion and absorption of nutrients, which can help alleviate symptoms like diarrhea, vomiting, and abdominal cramps.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, of the ginger plant is commonly used in traditional medicine due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making it effective in soothing digestive issues associated with gastroenteritis.

Roots: Ginger roots are a rich source of essential oils and bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat digestive disorders, including gastroenteritis, by reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms such as nausea and vomiting.
remedy_recipe: Gather 250 grams of fresh ginger root and grind it into a fine paste using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Mix the ginger paste with 100 milliliters of coconut oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler for 15 minutes.
Add 10 drops of peppermint essential oil and 5 drops of lemon essential oil to the mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Heat the mixture over low heat for an additional 30 minutes or until it reaches a consistency suitable for creaming with a spatula.
Pour the warm cream into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool before refrigerating for at least 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm creams helps with gastroenteritis because of its soothing and protective properties. The mucilages present in slippery elm bind to irritants in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with gastroenteritis. By forming a protective barrier on the gut lining, slippery elm cream can help calm irritated tissues, reduce inflammation, and promote healing. Mucilages: The mucilage content in slippery elm cream forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes of the digestive tract, soothing and calming irritated tissues, and helping to repair any damage caused by inflammation or infection.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried ulmus rubra bark and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 24 hours.
Combine the powdered ulmus rubra bark with 20ml of distilled water in a small saucepan and heat gently for 10-15 minutes over low heat.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it steep for an additional 30 minutes to allow the mixture to cool and infuse fully.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids, and set aside to cool completely within 2 hours.
Mix the cooled infusion with 20g of beeswax, 10ml of sweet almond oil, and 5ml of coconut oil in a double boiler, heating until the mixture reaches a smooth, consistent texture after 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves at dawn or mid-morning for maximum potency and quantity.

Grind dried peppermint leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle, achieving a yield of 100g within 30 minutes.
Melt beeswax (20g) and coconut oil (80g) in a double boiler at a temperature range of 120°f to 140°f for 1 hour.
Mix the ground peppermint powder with the melted oil mixture, stirring until uniform and maintaining a consistency suitable for cream application within 15 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean glycyrrhiza glabra roots then dry them in shade for two days.
Grind the dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Combine one part of powdered glycyrrhiza glabra root with three parts of coconut oil and heat mixture at 60 degrees celsius.
Stir the mixture until it cools then whip in beeswax and shea butter for emulsion preparation.
Pour the final cream into sterilized containers filling them to the top and seal tightly for storage.

remedy_recipe: Grind 10 grams of dried turmeric root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix the ground turmeric with 50 milliliters of warm coconut oil in a heatproof bowl, stirring until well combined for 3 minutes.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at a low temperature of 60 degrees celsius for 10 minutes, allowing the essential oils to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another heatproof bowl, squeezing out as much oil as possible from the turmeric solids for 5 minutes.
Allow the infused oil to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before transferring it to an airtight container for storage.

Steam distill the mixture at 100°c for 4 hours to extract essential oils and resins.
Combine extracted liquid with beeswax and shea butter in a ratio of 1:0.5:0.25 by weight.
Heat the mixture at 65°c for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully melted.
Pour the cooled cream into sterilized containers and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with gastroenteritis because they contain antimicrobial properties that soothe and calm the digestive system. The herbal extract in thyme cream has been shown to reduce inflammation, ease cramping, and alleviate diarrhea. Additionally, thyme's antispasmodic effects help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and discomfort associated with gastroenteritis. By applying thyme cream topically, individuals can experience fast relief from symptoms, allowing for a faster recovery time and reduced duration of illness.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help combat bacterial overgrowth in the gut, reducing symptoms like diarrhea and abdominal pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms like bloating, cramps, and nausea.
Antispasmodic properties: Thyme's antispasmodic compounds, such as terpinen-4-ol, help relax smooth muscle contractions in the gut, reducing symptoms like abdominal cramping and spasms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of thyme contain a high concentration of volatile oils, which provide anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties essential for soothing digestive issues.
Rhyzomes: Thyme rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat gastrointestinal disorders due to their ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and wash them thoroughly in cold running water.
Combine the washed thymus vulgaris leaves with 2 cups of distilled water in a saucepan and bring mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat and simmer for 20 minutes allowing the thymus vulgaris to infuse its active compounds into the water.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove solids and discard the solids retaining the infused liquid.
Combine the infused liquid with 1 cup of beeswax and 0.25 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat until the mixture reaches a temperature of 160 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock creams helps with gastroenteritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The cream's active ingredients, such as inulin and mucilage, soothe and calm irritated gastrointestinal tissues, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with diarrhea, stomach cramps, and nausea. Additionally, burdock's antimicrobial properties help to eliminate harmful bacteria that may be contributing to the infection, promoting a faster recovery and alleviating symptoms of gastroenteritis.
remedy_properties: Inulin: Inulin helps to soothe and calm inflamed gastrointestinal tissues, reducing symptoms of diarrhea, nausea, and abdominal cramps by promoting the growth of beneficial gut bacteria.
Tannins: Tannins in burdock cream have astringent properties that help to reduce inflammation and slow down diarrhea by binding to mucous membranes and reducing excess moisture in the gut.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive tract, alleviating symptoms of gastroenteritis like abdominal pain and cramping.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in inulin, a prebiotic fiber that helps regulate digestion and soothe irritated gut lining.
Buds: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest arctium lappa roots and leaves in mid-summer for optimal potency and quality.
Dry the plant material using a dehydrator at 55°c for 24 hours or air drying for 7 days.
Grind the dried arctium lappa roots and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 20g of the powdered herb with 100ml of sweet almond oil and 50ml of beeswax in a double boiler at 70°c for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat let cool and whip until smooth and creamy with an electric mixer for 5 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for gastroenteritis are topical preparations containing natural ingredients such as aloe vera, chamomile, and peppermint that provide relief from stomach discomfort, diarrhea, and abdominal pain associated with this condition. These creams work by soothing the digestive tract, reducing inflammation, and easing symptoms like cramping and bloating. Examples of herbal creams that help with gastroenteritis include Aloe Pura Anti-Inflammatory Cream and Nature's Bounty Peppermint Oil Cream. By using these creams, individuals can experience rapid relief from symptoms, improved digestion, and enhanced overall well-being, ultimately improving their quality of life.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat gastroenteritis with herbal creams is by using soothing and anti-inflammatory ingredients. Try applying topical creams containing aloe vera, chamomile, or calendula to the stomach area to reduce inflammation and discomfort. Additionally, incorporate herbal teas like peppermint and ginger into your diet to ease digestion and alleviate symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to gastroenteritis that are treated with herbal creams are inflammatory bowel disease, irritable bowel syndrome (IBS), and peptic ulcers. Herbal creams containing ingredients like aloe vera, chamomile, and turmeric have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe irritated mucous membranes and reduce inflammation in the digestive tract. These creams may also contain antiseptic agents to prevent infection and promote healing.
